items.title: Rockliff faces parliamentary grilling after Ogilvie resigns as minister

children.content: "You can ask it in 50 different ways … I won't be commenting any further", the Tasmanian premier says as he responds to questioning in parliament from Labor and the Greens.

articles.title: The end came quickly for Madeleine Ogilvie, who jumped before being pushed

children.content: It started with a seemingly innocuous question. Over the course of several months, Madeleine Ogilvie had many opportunities to provide clarification, but didn't until last week. Then the end came quickly.

items.title: Could native forests be part of Cement Australia's 'sustainable' fuel option?

children.content: Used tyres and wood waste will soon help to power a large cement manufacturing site in Tasmania as Cement Australia moves away from coal to alternative fuels, but some remain concerned the environmental benefit may be offset if the wood waste is sourced from logged native forests.

image.altText: Building Materias

items.title: Police 'open' to learning from volunteers who found remains in renewed search

children.content: A renewed search by volunteers for missing Tasmanian man Peter Willoughby led to the discovery of remains on Saturday. It's the second time this year a group of volunteers has made a significant find during the search for a missing person.

image.altText: A group of people wearing high visibility clothing search in a line, walking through dry eucalypt forest in the mist.

items.title: LISTEN: ME Bank customers report issues with accessing money and contacting bank for help

children.content: Some customers of ME Bank have reported issues with accessing their money and not being able to make contact with the bank to fix them.

gallery.altText: The ME Bank logo, lowercase me in a circle with a smile below it.

thumbnail.altText: The ME Bank logo, lowercase me in a circle with a smile below it.

items.title: How Mr Skelly offers 'vulnerable connection' to love, death and grief

children.content: An animation series tackling themes of grief, regret, love and gratitude has attracted an online fanbase of millions — and reflects the artist's search for peace and beauty.

image.altText: NOLh_Skellythumb3_2505_63

items.title: Police unlawfully arrested this man, broke his ribs, then charged him with assault

children.content: After fracturing about a dozen of a man's ribs and puncturing his lung, police officers discussed how to explain what happened to him.

gallery.altText: A man wearing a white polo with blue stripes is pushed into a holding cell by a police officer.
A man sits outdoors near a park, he looks ahead with a neutral expression. He is wearing a cap and a football jersey.

items.title: Why is Labor spending its political majority on this tax reform?

children.content: Why has Labor decided to spend the political capital of a large majority on a minor change to capital gains tax on businesses?

items.title: Sydney and Melbourne property values drop as housing market stalls in May

children.content: Australia's housing market continues to lose momentum, with national home values flatlining in May as higher interest rates, weak confidence and proposed property tax changes weigh on demand.

image.altText: Houses in Brisbane street

items.title: Almost 70,000 without power after storms tear through southern WA

children.content: Tens of thousands of homes are without power and the SES receives hundreds of calls for help, with the roof torn off one Perth apartment building, after WA's southern half is hammered by more severe weather.

items.title: Olympic stadium work underway as premier warns off protesters

children.content: The last protesters were removed from Victoria Park in the early hours of Monday morning.

image.altText: David Crisafulli and Jarrod Bleijie surrounded by children in AFL uniforms lifting shovels with dirt on them.

items.title: ASX lower despite Wall St gains, AUD flat, oil higher

children.content: The Australian share market has begun winter with a slight decline, with the ASX 200 in negative territory to start the new trading week. The weakness comes despite a solid finish on Wall Street on Friday, with the Dow Jones, S&P 500 and Nasdaq all ending higher. Follow our live business blog.

image.altText: Pedestrians are seen reflected on the Australian Stock Exchange trading board in Sydney

items.title: 'I won't be a yes man': Co-chair of ACT Reconciliation council resigns

children.content: ACT Reconciliation Council co-chair Selina Walker resigns, saying she has lost faith in the territory government's approach to Indigenous affairs.

items.title: Three-year-old child dies in Melbourne house fire

children.content: A child has died in a house fire in Werribee and another person has been taken to hospital with serious injuries.

items.title: Public servant to take SA government to court over golf course redevelopment

children.content: An Adelaide man alleges the state government failed to properly refer the controversial North Adelaide Golf Course to the federal environment minister for approval.

items.title: One-third of Ian Thorpe's life was in the spotlight, and it took its toll

children.content: Ian Thorpe became a global star as a teenager, attracting both opportunities and scrutiny. Now, he wishes he'd done some things differently.

items.title: Roadside stalls are booming, with 45,000 members in one group alone

children.content: Nestled away in the Adelaide Hills are dozens of roadside stalls dedicated to local produce, including baked goods and bouquets, and business is booming.

items.title: 'Bittersweet': These unique trees only flower once in their life — then die

children.content: About 20 Gebang palms have flowered in Darwin in a rare spectacle, reaffirming the local council's plans to foster a new generation of the trees.

items.title: 'Loving' parents not at fault for deaths of children left in hot cars, coroner finds

children.content: The deaths of two children who were left in cars during hot weather were the result of their parents' memory failure, a NSW coroner finds.

image.altText: Child's arm dangles while in a car seat.

items.title: House fire allegedly started by half-brother of murdered teen Angus Beaumont

children.content: A fire that destroyed a Redcliffe home and damaged two other was allegedly started by the half-brother of murdered teenager Angus Beaumont.

items.title: Melbourne Fringe board rejects artists' request for 'fair cut'

children.content: An open letter to festival organisers that attracted almost 600 signatures asked them to "give artists a fair cut", with specific requests to change the way fees are calculated.

image.altText: A crowed of people under purple and white jellyfish-like lanterns.

items.title: Two new faces named as Socceroos reveal World Cup squad

children.content: Recently realigned Cristian Volpato and Japan-born Tete Yengi are in line for potential national debuts after being named in Australia's squad for this year's World Cup.

image.altText: Tony Popovic addresses the Socceroos squad during a training session

items.title: AI videos spread 'wrong impression' of WA's north, tourism operators say

children.content: A wave of AI-generated videos posted to social media causes a stir among tour operators in WA's north. They say the content does not accurately portray the region and threatens the preservation of Indigenous culture.

gallery.altText: Bright blue water with rocks in the foreground.
This AI-generated video claims to show the Kimberley's Devonian limestone reef, with "sunbathing crocodiles".

This AI-generated video claims to show the Kimberley's Devonian limestone reef, with "sunbathing crocodiles".

items.title: Italian and Brazilian authorities investigate possible Ebola cases as outbreak grows

children.content: The true reach of the Ebola outbreak in central Africa could be much wider than the official figures published by central African authorities, the World Health Organization is warning.

items.title: Supreme leader hard to find as Trump wants peace plan rewrites

children.content: Donald Trump is reported to be amending a peace plan, which could fuel further delays in finding a final agreement.

items.title: Australia could still buy five US submarines if needed, defence minister says

children.content: Defence Minister Richard Marles has defended a shake-up to AUKUS that would involve Australia buying three second-hand Virginia-class submarines from the United States.

image.altText: Richard Marles speaks during an AUKUS Defence ministers' press huddle

items.title: Netanyahu orders deeper incursion into Lebanon as Israeli troops seize castle

children.content: Israeli troops have captured a strategic mountain topped with a Crusader-built castle in southern Lebanon, in their deepest incursion into the country in decades.

image.altText: Three soldiers stand looking at an old crumbling structure in the near distance

items.title: Why Naomi Osaka and Taylor Townsend's dinner party 'caused a stir'

children.content: From the Harlem Renaissance of the 20s and 30s to Soul Food Sundays at Wimbledon in the 80s and 90s, Naomi Osaka and Taylor Townsend's dinner party before the French Open continues a long history of black athletes coming together.

gallery.altText: Coco Gauff, Gael Monfils, Naomi Osaka, Taylor Townsend, Asia Muhammad and Christopher Eubanks stand arm in arm.
Tennis players Naomi Osaka and Taylor Townsend laugh together.

items.title: Quietly published Liberal Party paper raises gender membership concerns

children.content: The Liberal Party has quietly published a paper that floats offering a cut price $10 digital membership to join the party, in an effort to offset its declining numbers.

image.altText: Angus Taylor

items.title: US Navy interested in Darwin ship lift, NT minister says

children.content: Northern Territory Infrastructure Minister Bill Yan says the United States Navy has expressed interest in using the controversial Darwin ship lift, which is expected to be operational next year.

children.content: The prominent environmentalist declares the sand mine project "an industrial nightmare" after visiting the area.

image.altText: Bob Brown photographed in Hobart, February 2017.

items.title: Shein, Kmart fight back against Qld retailer in intellectual property dispute

children.content: Shein and Kmart have fired back at Sabo Skirt in an intellectual property dispute, saying the designs have been accused of copying are substantially similar to pre-existing designs.

items.title: Outback council hopes sorghum crop will reduce pressure on ratepayers

children.content: An outback Queensland council grows sorghum in a bid to create new income streams, reduce pressure on ratepayers, and support jobs in the region.
